macro_segments.title: The Question of Why Mother Is Not Included in the Fifteen Forbidden Relationships

micro_segments.title: Levi's Question to Rabbi: Why Doesn't the Mishnah List the Case of Mother?
The Debate Between Rabbi Yehuda and the Rabbanan Regarding Anusat Aviv
Rabbi's Response: The Mishnah Deals Only with Undisputed Cases, Not Disputes

macro_segments.title: The Case of a Brother's Wife When One Brother Was Not Yet Born

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Case of Eshet Achiv Shelo Haya Be'olamo
Scenario: Reuven Dies, Shimon Performs Yibum, Levi Is Born
The Status of Rivka as a Sister-in-Law Without a Yibum Obligation
Rabbi Shimon's Position: Different Scenarios Based on Yibum Timing
The Gemara's Resolution: Scenarios Where All Agree Versus Rabbi Shimon's Dispute

macro_segments.title: The Principle That All Fifteen Arayot Can Apply to Scenarios of Permitted and Forbidden Relationships Among Sisters

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's Statement: The Rule of 'Forbidden to One, Permitted to Another' Applies to All Fifteen
Scenario of Four Brothers: Two Married to Sisters Must Both Perform Chalitzah
How the Permitted-Forbidden Asymmetry Works: Freeing One Brother to Perform Yibum
The Concept of Bekulan: Finding Scenarios Where Each of the Fifteen Can Be Forbidden to One Brother and Permitted to Another
Example of Bito: Daughter as Forbidden to One Brother but Permitted to Another
The Complication: Daughters Must Be Sisters Require Non-Marital Union with Their Mother

macro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda's Restriction: Excluding Cases Arising from Non-Marital Union

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda Metargem: Only the Six Variations from Chamoto Downward Should Be Included
Reason: The Case of Bito Can Only Arise from Non-Marital Union, Not from Marriage

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Objection: The Principle Applies Even to Cases Arising from Non-Marital Union

micro_segments.title: Abaye Metargem: Even Bito from Anusat Aviv Should Be Included in Bekulan
Abaye's Exclusion: Eshet Achiv Shelo Haya Be'olamo Cannot Be Applied Symmetrically
The Problem: This Case Works Only According to Rabbi Shimon, Not the Rabbanan

macro_segments.title: Rav Safra's Position: All Fifteen Arayot, Including Eshet Achiv Shelo Haya Be'olamo, Can Apply According to Rabbi Shimon

micro_segments.title: Rav Safra Metargem: Even Eshet Achiv Shelo Haya Be'olamo Meshachat Lah According to Rabbi Shimon
The Mnemonic: Met, Noseh, Veyibem, Met, Noseh, Veyibem—the Sequence of Events
Scenario with Six Brothers: How Rabbi Shimon Allows the Asymmetry for Eshet Achiv Shelo Haya Be'olamo
Detailed Walkthrough: Reuven Dies, Yissachar Born, Levi Performs Yibum, Shimon Dies, Zevulun Born
Why This Works for Rabbi Shimon but Not the Rabbanan: The Role of When Yibum Occurs

macro_segments.title: Resolution of the Original Question: Why Mother Is Not Listed Despite Dealing with Disputes

micro_segments.title: The Core Issue: If Bekulan Includes Disputes, Why Not Include Anusat Aviv?
Rav Ada Karchina's Answer: Rabbi Can Hold Bekulan and Still Exclude Mother Because Anusat Aviv Cannot Work Symmetrically
The Impossibility: How Mother as Anusat Aviv Cannot Produce Sisters Where One Is Forbidden and One Is Permitted
Why the Scenario Fails: If the Daughters Are Sisters, They Cannot Marry Different Brothers Due to Mother's Sister Prohibition
Conclusion: Mother Cannot Be Included in the List Because She Cannot Satisfy the Conditions of Bekulan

macro_segments.title: Alternative Explanation: Mother Is Listed in the Next Mishnah of Forbidden Women, Indicating a Rabbi Yehuda Attribution

micro_segments.title: The Next Mishnah: Six Arayot More Severe Because They Can Never Be Married to a Brother
Mother Listed There: This Indicates the Mishnah Follows Rabbi Yehuda Who Forbids Anusat Aviv
Why Mother Is on the Severe List: Only According to Rabbi Yehuda Can Mother Never Be Married to a Brother
Rabbi's Dismissal of Levi: It Should Be Obvious from the Next Mishnah Why Mother Is Not Here

macro_segments.title: Ravina's Challenge: Even According to Rabbi Yehuda, a Scenario Can Be Found Where Mother Is Married to a Brother

micro_segments.title: Ravina's First Scenario: Be-di-avad, Rabbi Yehuda Allows a Binding Marriage to Anusat Aviv
Dismissal of Di-avad: We Discuss Le-chatchila, Not Transgressions
Ravina's Second Scenario: Yaakov Commits Adultery with Reuven's Wife, Producing a Son with His Own Mother
The Setup: Reuven Marries Rachel, Yaakov Commits Adultery, Yosef Is Born (a Mamzer)
After Reuven's Death: Yosef Has a Yibum Obligation with His Own Mother, Rachel
Rabbi Kahana's Rebuttal: We Discuss Relationships That Arose from Permitted Union, Not Prohibited Union

macro_segments.title: Levi's Alternative Mishnah: Expanding the List to Sixteen to Include Mother as Anusat Aviv

micro_segments.title: Levi Inserted Mother into His Version of the Mishnah: Sometimes She Permits the Co-Wife, Sometimes Not
Scenario One: Mother as Father's Wife Can Never Be Married to a Brother
Scenario Two: Mother as Anusat Aviv Can Be Married to a Brother in a Yibum Situation
Levi's Conclusion: Although the Rabbis Established Fifteen, a Sixteenth Case of Mother Is Possible

Bekulan (All Fifteen Cases Apply to Symmetric Scenarios)
Eshet Achiv Shelo Haya Be'olamo (Brother's Wife When Brother Not Yet Born)
Rabbi Yehuda's Position on Anusat Aviv
Scenario of Four Brothers with Two Sisters
Heter vs. Isur (Permitted vs. Prohibited Union)
